senses.definition: to start doing something quickly and energetically

examples.x: The team was motivated to belt into the fundraising campaign.
He couldn't wait to belt into his new role at the company.
The athletes were ready to belt into training for the upcoming competition.
She decided to belt into her homework right after school.
They planned to belt into the renovation project immediately.

senses.definition: to suddenly and intensely begin to express a particular emotion or reaction

examples.x: He burst into applause as soon as the performance ended.
The audience burst into laughter at the comedian’s joke.
They burst into cheers when their team scored the winning goal.
She burst into tears when she heard the news.
The baby burst into tears when her toy was taken away.
The children burst into giggles during the funny part of the story.

senses.definition: (of a lot of people or animals) to attempt to fit into a densely packed environment or a limited space

examples.x: As the doors opened, passengers started to cram into the already crowded subway car during rush hour.
Fans eagerly crammed into the concert venue, excited to see their favorite band perform.
The students rushed to cram into the small classroom for the popular guest lecture.
People from neighboring villages would cram into the small town square for the annual festival.
Tourists began to cram into the historic museum, eager to explore its exhibits.

senses.definition: to immerse oneself fully and enthusiastically into a particular activity, subject, or experience

examples.x: With the start of the new semester, students were ready to dive into their studies and pursue academic excellence.
The author encouraged readers to dive into the rich narrative of the novel, exploring its intricate characters and plot.
As the music played, the crowd began to dance, eager to dive into the lively atmosphere of the concert.
The scientist couldn't wait to dive into the research project, exploring uncharted territories in the field.
Upon arriving at the tropical destination, vacationers were excited to dive into the crystal-clear waters of the ocean.

senses.definition: to begin or become involved in a particular state, situation, agreement, or relationship

examples.x: The two companies decided to enter into a strategic partnership to expand their market reach.
The nations agreed to enter into negotiations to address diplomatic tensions.
Couples often choose to enter into marriage after careful consideration and mutual understanding.
It's important to read and understand the terms before you enter into any contractual agreement.

senses.definition: to begin doing something, often without prior intention

examples.x: During the vacation, I unexpectedly fell into the habit of waking up early to watch the sunrise.
After attending a painting workshop, Jane fell into the hobby of creating colorful abstract art.
Tom, with no prior plans, fell into a deep conversation with a stranger at the coffee shop.
Sarah didn't plan to adopt a healthier lifestyle, but she gradually fell into the routine of regular exercise and balanced eating.
Inspired by a documentary, the group of friends fell into the practice of volunteering at the local animal shelter every weekend.

senses.definition: to eagerly and energetically start doing something

examples.x: The team flung into preparations for the upcoming competition, eager to win.

senses.definition: to begin participating in, learning about, and developing a strong interest or passion for a particular activity, hobby, or topic

examples.x: They got into gardening and created a colorful backyard oasis.
He got into competitive sports at a young age.
After reading that book, he got into the author's works.
She got into cooking and started experimenting with new recipes.

senses.definition: to start doing something suddenly and with great enthusiasm

examples.x: The children launched into a game of tag.
The politician launched into a passionate speech about the need for change.
The professor launched into a long lecture on the history of the universe.
The company launched into a new era of innovation.
Every morning, she launches into her work with energy and determination.

senses.definition: to start a particular activity or task with great enthusiasm or vigor
